;<span id="Topic">topic</span>
:[[Glossary:Element|element]] that presents a synthetic overview of knowledge (e.g. an article to read). Knowledge stored in '''topics''' is gradually converted into [[Glossary:Item|items]] (e.g. in the process of [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]]). Optimally, '''topics''' introduce you to the learned knowledge by providing a synthetic overview. You later keep the knowledge in your memory by only reviewing [[Glossary:Item|items]]. In a well-structured [[Glossary:Collection|collection]], '''topics''' will always be [[Glossary:Parent|parents]] to [[Glossary:Item|items]] derived from their contents. Each time a student loses the sense of context during [[Glossary:Repetition|repetition]], he or she can press ''Ctrl+Up'' to view the [[Glossary:Parent|parent]] of the current [[Glossary:Element|element]]. This way a quick review of the synthetic material in the '''topic''' is possible. See also: [[Items, topics, concepts, and tasks|Topics vs items]]
